_label_note: SA Stage A strict tier reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine after Codex/GPT-assisted mismatch review; broader model-derived labels retained in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. Narrow fixes covered Secretariat role-mailbox bait, public/label-only phone bait, CR/VAT/National Address/Iqama numeric fragments, URL CR/NID/Iqama fragments, Hijri Sha-ban date bait, public MOU wording, and training-roster insider-list wording. Generated placeholder/invalid KSA National ID, Iqama, and CR labels were removed from strict must_detect when current deterministic strict mode suppresses them.
_label_tier_note: SA Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Stage B labels are project-owner approved for internal benchmarking.

must_detect.matched_text:
This memo is confidential under CMA Market Conduct Regulations and internal to the Board, Legal, and Clean Energy desk; do not circulate externally.
Falaj Power JSC (CR 2043321990) is evaluating a private PPA and a climate-linked sukuk, with draft Scope 1–3 emissions baselines and verifier comments not yet disclosed on Saudi Exchange (Tadawul), and therefore treated as MNPI.
Concurrently, the issuer’s OT vendor, Vault Oasis WLL, reported a cyber incident on 28 Sha'ban 1448H involving suspected credential theft and lateral movement; no public notice has been made and this remains non-public operational risk information.
Mr. Yasser Al-Hadidi (CFO, Iqama 2087319456) shared bank term sheets reflecting a 135 bps sustainability margin ratchet and a 5-year SAIBOR cap with step-down triggers; these commercial terms are MNPI until an approved Tadawul announcement is posted.
Preliminary conversations with Nafith Digital Assets LLC (CR 1017782209) on a tokenized REC/crypto custody pilot under the CMA FinTech Lab are exploratory only and must stay within the Clean Energy desk; no outreach to counterparties or media.
Information barriers: segregate Renewables Origination from Hanifa Trading; restrict shared drives and chat channels; add Mr. Al-Hadidi and Ms. Reem Al-Qadi (Company Secretary) to the insider list effective 15 Dhul-Qadah 1448H.
Blackout window: prohibit Falaj insiders and Hanifa deal team from trading Falaj Power securities or derivatives from 15 Dhul-Qadah 1448H until two full trading days after cyber and financing disclosures appear on Tadawul.
Selective disclosure/tipping: route all external queries to Investor Relations; breaches may trigger CMA sanctions for unlawful disclosure and market abuse.
PDPL handling: Mr. Al-Hadidi’s Iqama (2087319456), mobile (+966-55-019-4472), and email y.alhadidi@falajpower.sa are personal data; store encrypted in KSA with retention of 3 years, and any cross-border processing requires a SDAIA-approved transfer mechanism.
No copies of vendor logs with device IDs, access tokens, or employee identifiers shall be exported to non-KSA cloud regions without a SDAIA transfer assessment and execution of SDAIA model contractual clauses with the processor.
Operations-only note: the internal training titled “Introduction to Crypto Custody Controls” uses public, anonymized examples and does not include client or issuer MNPI.
Marketing caution: draft materials claiming “market-leading ESG performance” must remove forward-looking claims until emissions re-baselining is finalized and attested by the external assurer.
Data minimization: remove national ID scans from the deal room; if indexing is required, retain only last 4 digits of Iqama and redact contact numbers from PDFs.
Incident response: Cyber team to provide a sanitized timeline to the Board Risk Committee; raw IOC lists may contain staff PII and vendor secrets and should remain restricted.
All insider list updates will be maintained by the Company Secretary and made available to CMA on request.
